title: verbascum collinum
intro: Verbascum collinum, also known as hill mullein, is a biennial or perennial plant native to Europe, characterized by its tall, upright stems and large, yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Verbascum collinum include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve respiratory function. Verbascum collinum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herb for herbal remedies and homeopathic treatments.
